frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: A: Kal function mein Riya ko dekha? Saree mein kitni decent lag rahi thi, bilkul apni sanskriti jaisi. B: Haan, warna aajkal ki ladkiyan itne western kapde pehen leti hain. A: Phir bolti hain log dekhte kyun hain, beta thoda maryada mein rehna bhi zaroori hota hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it links a woman's clothing to her decency and respectability, while subtly blaming women for attention or harassment based on what they wear. It encodes the traditional-dress-as-superior compliment and the clothing-harassment link.
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 48. Married for 24 years. A woman who worked for six years before stopping when her first child was born. You are at a family function sitting with three other women your age. One woman mentions that her daughter in law has just gone back to work full time when her child is only eight months old. The conversation opens up. You are giving your opinion on women who return to work this early and what it means for the child and what you think women of this generation have confused about what ambition is supposed to look like and what you gave up and whether you regret it.
